Name: Barnwell Waste Management Facility
Notes: *Owned by the State of South Carolina, operated by Chem-Nuclear Systems (EnergySolutions subsidiary)
Timeline Notes: An older NRC document says the site lease expires in 2075, but a lease-expiration date is not necessarily a planned operational closure date.

Name: U.S. Ecology Washington
Planned Closure Year: 2056 — planned start of final closure activities
Timeline Notes: Its 2004 closure planning basis assumed closure would start in 2056, take approximately seven years, and then enter at least 100 years of institutional control.  This plan is subject to revision.

Name: EnergySolutions Clive Operations
Timeline Notes: EnergySolutions has published closure plans and financial-assurance requirements, but no credible current source establishing a year for final closure.

Name: Area 5 Radioactive Waste Management Complex
Notes: Must be DOE-related or otherwise approved under the NNSS Waste Acceptance Criteria
Timeline Notes: Individual cells are constructed and closed as needed, and there is no publicly established year for closure of the entire Area 5 complex.

Name: E-Area Low-Level Waste Facility – Savannah River Site
Timeline Notes: Savannah River National Laboratory documentation states that E-Area is projected to remain in operation until 2065.

Name: Texas Compact Waste Disposal Facility – WCS
Notes: Principally Texas and Vermont, but approved waste may be accepted by Texas regulators
Timeline Notes: There is no fixed closure year found in current public licensing material.

Name: Federal Waste Disposal Facility – WCS
Notes: Principally federal LLW, but has separate authorized inventory for Texas Compact
